response.justification: The role demands 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum plus deep large-scale infrastructure and cloud-compute-platform experience, and this candidate's C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments and a university thesis — nowhere near the requirement. The background is strong in web/full-stack work (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with some distributed-systems and AWS/Kubernetes exposure, but it does not align with a C++-centric internal compute platform technical-lead position. Without the core language depth and formal cloud/DevOps technical-leadership track record, this is a poor match despite a long and varied career.
strengths: Distributed/low-latency systems experience (microservices handling millions of req/sec at Sociomantic)
Long, broad engineering career with staff/senior titles and mentoring via workshops and courses
Some cloud and infra exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD)
concerns: C++ experience is ~6 months plus a thesis, far below the required 8 years
No production C++ large-scale infrastructure or compute-platform work
No clear formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
